'''Michael "Mick"/"Mike" Smith''' (born {{circa|1947}}) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1960s and 1970s. He played at representative level for [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]], and at club level for [[Featherstone Rovers]], and [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], as a {{rlp|ce}}, or {{rlp|so}}.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/Mick-Smith/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}{{dead link|date=January 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref>

==Background==
Michael Smith was born in [[Rossington]], [[Doncaster]], [[West Riding of Yorkshire]], England.

==Playing career==

===County honours===
Michael Smith won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Yorkshire rugby league team|Yorkshire]] while at [[Featherstone Rovers]]; during the [[1974–75 Northern Rugby Football League season|1974–75 season]] against [[Cumbria rugby league team|Cumbria]] and [[Other Nationalities rugby league team|Other Nationalities]].

===Challenge Cup Final appearances===
Mick Smith played {{rlp|so}} in [[Featherstone Rovers]]' 17-12 victory over [[Barrow Raiders|Barrow]] in the [[1966–67 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1966–67 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1966–67 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 13 May 1967, in front of a crowd of 76,290, played at {{rlp|ce}} (replaced by [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] [[David Hartley (rugby league)|David "Dave" Hartley]]) in the 33-14 victory over [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford Northern]] in the [[1972–73 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1972–73 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1972–73 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 12 May 1973, in front of a crowd of 72,395, and played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 9-24 defeat by [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[1973–74 Challenge Cup]] Final during the [[1973–74 Northern Rugby Football League season#Challenge Cup|1973–74 season]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 11 May 1974, in front of a crowd of 77,400.

===County Cup Final appearances===
Mick Smith played {{rlp|so}} in [[Featherstone Rovers]]' 12-25 defeat by [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[1966–67 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1966–67 Northern Rugby Football League season|1966–67 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 15 October 1966,<ref name="Yorkshire Cup 1966/67">{{cite web|url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/competitions/Yorkshire_Cup_1966/Final/Featherstone_Rovers-vs-Hull_Kingston_Rovers/summary.html|title=Yorkshire Cup 1966/67|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|access-date=1 January 2012}}</ref> played at {{rlp|ce}} in the 9-12 defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[1969–70 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1969–70 Northern Rugby Football League season|1969–70 season]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]], [[Leeds]] on Saturday 20 September 1969, and played on the {{rlp|wg}} in the 7-23 defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[1970–71 Yorkshire Cup]] Final during the [[1970–71 Northern Rugby Football League season|1970–71 season]] at [[Odsal Stadium]], [[Bradford]] on Saturday 21 November 1970.

===Club career===
Mick Smith made his début for [[Featherstone Rovers]] on Saturday 31 October 1964, and he played his last match for Featherstone Rovers during the [[1976–77 Northern Rugby Football League season|1976–77 season]].<ref name="Bailey, Ron 20 September 2001">Bailey, Ron (20 September 2001). ''Images of Sport - Featherstone Rovers Rugby League Football Club''. The History Press. {{ISBN|0752422952}}</ref>

===Career records===
The Featherstone Rovers most [[Try (rugby)|tries]] in a match record of six tries is jointly held by; [[Chris Bibb]], [[Brad Dwyer]], and Mike Smith, Smith scored six tries against [[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]] on Saturday 13 April 1968.<ref>{{cite web |title=Records |url=http://www.featherstonerovers.net/page.php?id=1097 |publisher=Featherstone Rovers RLFC |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100405013434/http://www.featherstonerovers.net/page.php?id=1097 |access-date=7 July 2014 |archive-date=2010-04-05}}</ref>

===Testimonial match===
Mick Smith's [[Benefit (sports)|benefit]] [[Benefit season|season]]/[[testimonial match]] at [[Featherstone Rovers]] took place during the [[1974–75 Northern Rugby Football League season|1974–75 season]].

==Coaching career==
Mick Smith has coached Doncaster Toll Bar Under-16s.

==Honoured at Featherstone Rovers==
Mick Smith is a Featherstone Rovers Hall of Fame inductee.<ref name="Featherstone Rovers legends added to Hall of Fame">{{cite web|url=http://www.pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk/news/featherstone-rovers-legends-added-to-hall-of-fame-1-8214653|title=Featherstone Rovers legends added to Hall of Fame|publisher=pontefractandcastlefordexpress.co.uk|date=5 November 2016|access-date=1 January 2017}}</ref>
